summary: Matched the common fighter StopWall code by moving several routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C translation unit, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_StopWall.c, with a matching report showing 100% for the file and all listed functions. The PR also added a dedicated StopWall header, wired the new object into obj_files.mk, improved related prototypes, and renamed a collision flag and conversion constants for clearer typed meaning.
what_changed: Removed the StopWall routines and related sdata2 constants from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s while leaving later ft_08A4 assembly in place.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_StopWall.c implementing ftCo_8009EDA4, ftCo_8009EE30, ftCo_StopWall_Anim, ftCo_StopWall_IASA, ftCo_StopWall_Phys, and ftCo_StopWall_Coll.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_StopWall.h declaring the non-static StopWall entry points.
Added ftCo_StopWall.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so the new C object is built before the remaining ft_08A4.s.o.

Changed the wall collision flag macro name from MPCOLL_FLAGS_B5 to MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05 in src/common_structs.h and updated users in Captain Falcon side-B collision, common damage collision, and wall jump code.
Renamed placeholder conversion constants CONV_LO and CONV_HI to U32_TO_F32 and S32_TO_F32, with visible updates to ftCo_Shouldered.c and ftSk_SpecialHi.c.
Improved function prototypes: ft_800843FC is now typed as void ft_800843FC(ftCo_GObj* gobj), and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C now takes an HSD_GObj* plus Vec3* argument.
Removed old StopWall declarations from ft_08A4.h after introducing the dedicated ftCo_StopWall.h.
Included ftCommon/ftCo_StopWall.h from ft_081B.c, indicating the matched StopWall entry point became part of typed C references there.
smart_moves: Kept ftCo_8009EE30 static in the new C file while exposing only the externally needed StopWall entry points in the new header, matching the old ft_08A4.h comment that ftCo_8009EE30 was static.

Used the existing CollData wall flag model directly in ftCo_8009EDA4: left-facing fighters check MPCOLL_FLAGS_B11 and right-facing fighters check MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05 before comparing ABS(gr_vel) against walk_max_vel.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout with MUST_MATCH-only unused stack padding in ftCo_8009EE30 instead of forcing artificial logic into normal builds.

Selected the collision correction vector from xA4_ecbCurrCorrect.left or .right depending on wall collision state, then passed that Vec3 to ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C before changing motion state to ftCo_MS_StopWall.
Converted raw assembly constants for -1.0, 1.0, and 0.0 into normal C literals and NULL arguments where appropriate, avoiding retained local sdata2 symbols for this matched slice.
Tightened ambiguous UNK prototypes while matching this code, reducing future callsite guesswork for ft_800843FC and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C.
decomp_lessons: When matching a small action-state file, move only the matched routines out of the larger asm file and keep the remaining asm object in the build; obj_files.mk ordering can support mixed C and asm during gradual decompilation.
Dedicated action headers such as ftCo_StopWall.h are preferable to accumulating unrelated declarations in broad numeric headers like ft_08A4.h once a subsystem has a coherent C translation unit.
Collision flag names should be zero-padded when the surrounding convention uses bit numbers such as B11 and B14; MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05 is clearer and sorts visually with other bit-index names.
Typed prototypes are often unlocked by matching callsites: discovering ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C takes a Vec3* and ft_800843FC takes ftCo_GObj* helps future fighter code avoid UNK_PARAMS.
Empty action callbacks can be represented as empty C functions for IASA and Phys when the original assembly is just blr.
Simple animation callbacks can match cleanly with the common pattern if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)) ft_8008A2BC(gobj).
Use MUST_MATCH guards for unused stack bytes when they are needed for binary identity but should not pollute normal semantics.
Renaming placeholder conversion doubles from generic CONV_LO/CONV_HI to U32_TO_F32/S32_TO_F32 documents the intended integer-to-float conversion trick and reduces ambiguity in unrelated files.
naming_conventions: New file and header follow the ftCommon action naming pattern: ftCo_StopWall.c and ftCo_StopWall.h.
Action callbacks use the ftCo_StopWall_Anim, ftCo_StopWall_IASA, ftCo_StopWall_Phys, and ftCo_StopWall_Coll names.
Unidentified but address-named functions retain ftCo_8009EDA4-style names until semantics are known.
Static helper ftCo_8009EE30 remains address-named and local to ftCo_StopWall.c.
Collision bit macro was normalized from MPCOLL_FLAGS_B5 to MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05 to align with B11 and B14 naming.
Placeholder conversion constants were renamed to semantic integer conversion names: U32_TO_F32 and S32_TO_F32.
Header guard uses the address-style form GALE01_09EDA4, matching common decomp project header guard style visible in the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted 146 lines of assembly from ft_08A4.s after replacing ftCo_8009EDA4, ftCo_8009EE30, and StopWall callbacks with C.
The PR report claims 100.00% for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_StopWall.c and for each listed function.
Used explicit float constants 0.0f, 1, and NULL in Fighter_ChangeMotionState to reproduce the motion-state call formerly assembled with ftCo_804D8788 and ftCo_804D8784.
Used ABS(fp->gr_vel) > ca->walk_max_vel to express the assembly's absolute-value comparison before entering StopWall.
Used a scoped Vec3 stack variable and MUST_MATCH-only padding around it to match stack behavior in ftCo_8009EE30.
Preserved the two-step behavior after motion-state change: adjust fp->cur_pos.x, then call ft_800843FC(gobj) and ftCommon_8007E2FC(gobj).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_8009EDA4 or ftCo_8009EE30 to semantic StopWall helper names.
Search for remaining MPCOLL_FLAGS_B5 references to verify the rename to MPCOLL_FLAGS_B05 was completed project-wide.
Inspect later changes to ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C to confirm the Vec3* parameter name and semantics.
Look for follow-up PRs that replace the asm implementation of ft_800843FC with C now that its prototype is typed.
Search for further CONV_LO or CONV_HI references to ensure U32_TO_F32 and S32_TO_F32 replaced the placeholder names consistently.
